questions.question: Which of the following is the correct terminology for the gap between two neurons where they communicate?
Which of the following is an example of plasticity in plants?
Which of the following minerals is essential for oxygen transport in the blood?
Which institute of Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) developed India’s first genome-edited rice variety DRR Rice 100 (Kamla)?
questions.option_1: ICAR-Central Institute of Agricultural Engineering, Bhopal
questions.option_3: ICAR-Indian Institute of Rice Research, Hyderabad
questions.option_4: ICAR-Indian Agricultural Research Institute, New Delhi

questions.question: Which of the following terms describe a change in the DNA sequence resulting in changes in the genotype and the phenotype of an organism?
What is the main function of sclerenchyma, a plant tissue composed of dead cells with extremely thick lignified walls?
questions.option_1: To provide mechanical support and strength to the plant
questions.option_2: To transport water, nutrients and sugars throughout the plant
questions.option_3: To store food and other materials such as resins, mucilage and latex in the plant
questions.option_4: To help hold the flower parts together in a plant

questions.question: In which year was the first cases of the disease now known as acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) identified?
The seeds of which plant of the Fabaceae family are used as a spice, flavouring agent and food supplement?
What is the main function of carbohydrates in the body?
What pigment do petunias have that gives them a reddish-purple colour in an acidic environment (low pH)?
Which mineral helps in the formation of insulin granules, digestion, metabolism and functioning of the male prostate?
Which pair of scientists first proposed the double-helix model for the structure of DNA?
Biological disasters are mainly caused by:
Which macromolecule is primarily responsible for energy storage in plants?
